title: Constructive With Claims and Impacts But No Causal Mechanism
topic: Resolved: The United States federal government should substantially reduce its military presence in East Asia.
transcript:
We negate the resolution. The United States should maintain its military presence in East Asia.

Contention One: Regional stability. American military bases provide regional stability. Without them, the region will become more dangerous. The impact is increased risk of conflict and instability across East Asia.

Contention Two: Alliance credibility. Our alliances depend on forward presence. If we withdraw, our allies will not trust us. The impact is that our alliance system will collapse and America will be isolated.

For these reasons, we urge a negative ballot.
notes: Isolated missing_warrant case. Both contentions assert a claim and state an impact but provide no causal mechanism explaining the connection. No named evidence. Intentionally avoids vague attribution phrases to isolate the missing_warrant signal from weak_evidence.
